behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is template-lint logic itself, not cross-module handoff. REAL-INFRA: none required; in-memory strings reproduce failure. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration (L+1): behavior says "static prompt-template lint check," which disqualifies needing module wiring/real files as minimum. Higher levels (System/Agentic/Workflow) are disqualified by absence of OS/network/model language.
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint function with a template string containing phase instruction text copied from SKILL.md and assert it returns a reject result with leakage location metadata.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears at module boundaries (template + interpolation + fixture assembly). REAL-INFRA: local wiring only; no real network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Unit (L-1): text "fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures)" requires real module composition. Rejected System (L+1): no requirement for OS/network/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run real harness prompt assembly with representative fixtures, inspect final prompt text, and fail if any phase-specific SKILL.md instruction leakage is present.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in handoff between invocation, file loader, and prompt builder; unit-only misses wiring defects. REAL-INFRA: local filesystem read is sufficient within integration scope. LLM-DEP: behavior can be validated by proving context source and prompt/input path, not model judgment quality. MULTI-STEP: no required agent chain. Rejected Unit (L-1): text "loads skill context from plugin/skill files" implies cross-module/file interaction. Rejected System (L+1): no real HTTP/subprocess/OS-dependent primitive beyond standard local file read.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the test harness end-to-end up to request construction, assert skill context is sourced from plugin/skill file contents (not embedded literals), and verify changing file contents changes loaded context.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires harness variant-selection + prompt immutability + file-source switching across modules. REAL-INFRA: local file variants are enough; no external system needed. LLM-DEP: this behavior is about invocation mechanics, not model output quality. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agent chain required. Rejected Unit (L-1): text "harness can run...against...variant" with unchanged prompt requires orchestration across loader/config modules. Rejected System (L+1): no real network/subprocess/OS-only failure boundary specified.
behaviors.test_description: Execute harness twice (baseline and degraded/missing SKILL.md variant) with identical user prompt text; assert run setup succeeds and prompt text remains byte-identical while loaded skill source differs.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: pass/fail depends on how the real model behavior changes from altered skill context; stubs would hide this. MOCK-HIDE: mocked model could return expected structure regardless of skill regression. MULTI-STEP: single prompt/response is sufficient; no required multi-agent sequence. Rejected Integration (L-1): text "behavioral test fails...under the same user-style prompt" after skill degradation requires real model judgment effect, not only wiring. Rejected Workflow (L+1): behavior does not require "multiple agentic steps in sequence"; only one live execution decision boundary is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Run live model test with baseline skill and degraded/removed relevant SKILL.md section using identical user-style prompt; assert baseline passes and degraded variant fails on behavior criteria.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is ordering across response capture, parser, and artifact writer modules. REAL-INFRA: local artifact write/read in-process integration is enough; no subprocess/network dependence. LLM-DEP: can use deterministic fixture response; correctness is sequencing, not model quality. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Unit (L-1): behavior spans multiple components (capture + parser + artifact sink). Rejected System (L+1): no requirement for real external infra beyond normal local file write.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a raw response through the harness pipeline and assert diagnostic artifact stores unparsed output before parser invocation (e.g., via call order/trace and artifact contents).

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real risk is test harness still wired to weak keyword-only assertions even if structural checker exists. REAL-INFRA: no external system required. LLM-DEP: can validate with crafted outputs; no real model needed for this behavior alone. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Unit (L-1): text "Assertions enforce...not only keyword presence" targets assertion stack behavior in the actual test path, not just a single helper function. Rejected Agentic (L+1): behavior concerns assertion semantics and wiring, not model-output quality dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Run behavioral assertion layer used by the harness on crafted outputs where keywords are present but schema/role placement is wrong; assert failure is triggered by structural rules.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: requires live model outputs where subtle structure can regress while keywords remain; deterministic stubs hide this failure class. MULTI-STEP: stated as live execution with assertion comparison, not multi-agent pipeline. REAL-INFRA: external API call may exist, but primary minimum boundary is model non-deterministic output quality. Rejected Integration (L-1): text "Under live model execution" disqualifies pure stubbed integration as minimum. Rejected Workflow (L+1): no concrete requirement for "multiple agentic steps in sequence" in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live model with user-style prompt and compare keyword-only vs structural assertions on returned output; confirm structural assertions fail a regression case that keyword checks would pass.
